name: always_minimum_index
code:
proof fn phi_1_always_minimum_index(va: usize)
    requires
        va_4k_valid(va) || va_2m_valid(va) || va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_v2l4index(va) == KERNEL_MEM_END_L4INDEX,
{
}
reason: If provable, the L4 index is always 1 and the function is degenerate, unable to address multiple L4 page-table entries.

name: upper_bound_unreachable
code:
proof fn phi_2_upper_bound_unreachable(va: usize)
    requires
        va_4k_valid(va) || va_2m_valid(va) || va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_v2l4index(va) < 0x1ff,
{
}
reason: If provable, the maximum L4 index 0x1ff is unreachable, meaning the ensures upper bound is vacuously loose and the top page-table slot is inaccessible.

name: lower_bound_unreachable
code:
proof fn phi_3_lower_bound_unreachable(va: usize)
    requires
        va_4k_valid(va) || va_2m_valid(va) || va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_v2l4index(va) > KERNEL_MEM_END_L4INDEX,
{
}
reason: If provable, the lower bound KERNEL_MEM_END_L4INDEX is never actually achieved, making the ensures lower bound vacuously loose.

name: result_always_even
code:
proof fn phi_4_result_always_even(va: usize)
    requires
        va_4k_valid(va) || va_2m_valid(va) || va_1g_valid(va),
    ensures
        spec_v2l4index(va) % 2 == 0,
{
}
reason: If provable, odd L4 indices are unreachable, halving the effective address space coverage of the page table.

name: injective_on_valid_addresses
code:
proof fn phi_5_injective_on_valid_addresses(va1: usize, va2: usize)
    requires
        va_4k_valid(va1) || va_2m_valid(va1) || va_1g_valid(va1),
        va_4k_valid(va2) || va_2m_valid(va2) || va_1g_valid(va2),
        va1 != va2,
    ensures
        spec_v2l4index(va1) != spec_v2l4index(va2),
{
}
reason: If provable, every distinct valid VA maps to a distinct L4 index, meaning each index covers at most one address — contradicting the purpose of page-table indexing which groups many addresses under one entry.
